steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states: ‘on average, studies of fish showed larger differences than studies of birds did, [but] for every class of animals examined, there were individual studies showing differences well above the average for fish.’ The blank must follow logically from both parts — fish lead on average, but some bird studies surpass even the fish average.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘fish studies were more likely than bird studies to specify whether effects were detrimental’: The passage never discusses whether studies reported effects as detrimental. This introduces information not in the text. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘differences are likely more pronounced for birds than for fish’: This reverses the passage. Fish showed larger differences on average — the opposite of what Choice B claims. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Wysocki’s difference was likely larger than the average difference for fish in the meta-analysis’: Wysocki studied common carp (fish), but the passage gives no information about how Wysocki’s specific result compared to the meta-analysis fish average. This is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘some studies of birds found larger effects than some studies of fish did’: If some bird studies exceeded the fish average, those bird studies necessarily surpassed all fish studies that were at or below the average — so some bird studies did find larger effects than some fish studies. This follows directly and necessarily from the passage. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
